subject: English
chapter: A Tale of Valour: Major Somnath Sharma and the Battle of Badgam
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – A Tale of Valour
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Major Somnath Sharma was a brave officer in the Indian Army. He belonged to the Kumaon Regiment and was known for his dedication and courage. Even from a young age, he believed in serving his country with honesty and discipline.
questions: Who was Major Somnath Sharma?
Which regiment did he belong to?
What qualities was he known for?
What did he believe in from a young age?
How did he want to serve his country?

passages.paragraph: In 1947, India faced a difficult situation in the region of Kashmir. Enemy forces attempted to capture important areas near Srinagar. Protecting the city and its airport became extremely important for the safety of the region.
questions: When did the conflict in Kashmir take place?
What did the enemy forces try to capture?
Which city was in danger?
Why was Srinagar airport important?
What became necessary for the safety of the region?

passages.paragraph: Major Somnath Sharma was assigned to lead a company of soldiers to Badgam near Srinagar. Their task was to patrol the area and observe enemy movements. The soldiers understood that their duty was critical for the defence of the city.
questions: Where was Major Somnath Sharma sent?
What was his task in Badgam?
What were the soldiers supposed to observe?
Why was their duty important?
Which city were they helping to defend?

passages.paragraph: At the time of the mission, Major Somnath Sharma had his arm in a plaster cast because of an injury. However, he refused to stay behind. He insisted on leading his soldiers because he believed that a leader must stand with his men in difficult situations.
questions: What injury did Major Somnath Sharma have?
What did he refuse to do?
What did he insist on doing?
Why did he want to lead his soldiers?
What did he believe about leadership?

passages.paragraph: Soon the soldiers realized that they were surrounded by a large number of enemy fighters. Although the enemy greatly outnumbered them, the soldiers continued to defend their position bravely. Major Somnath Sharma encouraged them to remain strong and fight with courage.
questions: What did the soldiers soon realize?
How many enemy fighters surrounded them?
Did the soldiers give up their position?
How did the soldiers defend themselves?
What did Major Somnath Sharma encourage them to do?

passages.paragraph: During the battle, Major Somnath Sharma sent a message to his headquarters. He said that the enemy was only fifty yards away and that they were heavily outnumbered. However, he also declared that his soldiers would fight to the last man and the last round.
questions: What message did Major Somnath Sharma send?
How close were the enemy forces?
What difficulty did the soldiers face?
What did he declare about the fight?
What does this message show about his courage?

passages.paragraph: The soldiers continued fighting bravely even under heavy attack. Their determination delayed the enemy advance toward Srinagar. This delay allowed more Indian troops to reach the area and strengthen the defence of the city.
questions: How did the soldiers continue fighting?
What did their determination delay?
Which city was protected because of this delay?
What arrived because of the delay?
How did the defence of the city become stronger?

passages.paragraph: During the intense fighting, a mortar shell exploded near Major Somnath Sharma. He was killed in the explosion while performing his duty. His sacrifice became a symbol of bravery and dedication for the Indian Army.
questions: What happened during the intense fighting?
Where did the mortar shell explode?
What happened to Major Somnath Sharma?
What was he doing at that time?
What did his sacrifice become a symbol of?

passages.paragraph: For his extraordinary courage and sacrifice, Major Somnath Sharma was awarded the Param Vir Chakra. This award is the highest military decoration for bravery in India. He became the first recipient of this honour.
questions: What award was given to Major Somnath Sharma?
Why was he given this award?
What is special about the Param Vir Chakra?
What position did he hold among the recipients?
What does the award recognize?

passages.paragraph: The story of Major Somnath Sharma teaches us the values of courage, patriotism, and sacrifice. His bravery reminds us that the safety of a nation often depends on the dedication of its soldiers. His life continues to inspire generations of Indians.
questions: What values does the story teach?
Whose bravery is described in the story?
What does the safety of a nation depend on?
Who continues to inspire generations?
Why is this story important for readers?
